title: Quiz: QNN Applications
description: Test your understanding of Quantum Neural Network applications including classification, regression, and generative modeling

questions.question: In a QNN binary classifier, how is the class probability typically obtained?

options.text: By computing the trace of the density matrix
By measuring the probability of a qubit being in the |1> state
By computing the fidelity with a reference state
By summing all amplitudes of the output state

questions.explanation: In QNN classification, the probability of class 1 is typically obtained by measuring a designated qubit and computing P(|1>), the probability of finding it in the |1> state.

questions.question: What is a Born machine in the context of quantum generative models?

options.text: A classical neural network inspired by quantum mechanics
A quantum circuit that generates samples according to |<x|U|0>|^2
A quantum error correction scheme for generative tasks
A hybrid model that combines GAN and quantum circuits

questions.explanation: A Born machine uses the Born rule of quantum mechanics: the probability of measuring outcome x is |<x|U(theta)|0>|^2, making the quantum state directly encode a probability distribution.

questions.question: For quantum regression, how is a continuous output value typically obtained from a QNN?

options.text: By counting the number of |1> measurements
By computing the expectation value of an observable like <Z>
By reading out all qubit amplitudes directly
By computing the von Neumann entropy

questions.explanation: QNN regression typically computes the expectation value of an observable (commonly <Z>), which produces a continuous value in [-1, 1] that can be scaled to the desired output range.

questions.question: Born machines can generate samples from arbitrary probability distributions without any limitation.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Born machines face practical limitations including the exponential cost of estimating the full distribution (requires many measurements), limited expressibility of the quantum circuit, and training challenges for complex distributions.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of using QNNs for analyzing quantum data?

options.text: QNNs are always faster than classical methods
QNNs can process quantum states directly without classical intermediate representation
QNNs require fewer qubits than the input quantum state
QNNs automatically correct errors in quantum data

questions.explanation: QNNs can process quantum states natively, avoiding the exponential cost of tomography to convert quantum data to classical representation. This is particularly valuable when the input is already quantum.

questions.question: When benchmarking QNNs against classical neural networks, which factor should NOT be the only comparison metric?

options.text: Final accuracy on test data
Number of trainable parameters
Sample complexity (training data needed)
All of these should be considered together

questions.explanation: Fair benchmarking requires considering multiple factors: accuracy, parameter count, sample complexity, computational resources, and the specific problem structure. No single metric captures the full picture.

questions.question: Which are valid loss functions for training QNN classifiers? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Binary cross-entropy loss
Mean squared error between predictions and labels
Hinge loss
KL divergence between output distribution and target

questions.explanation: All listed loss functions can be used for QNN classification: cross-entropy is standard for probabilistic outputs, MSE works with probability predictions, hinge loss for SVM-style training, and KL divergence for distributional matching.

questions.question: In data re-uploading for classification, why is the data encoded multiple times throughout the circuit?

options.text: To correct encoding errors
To increase the effective expressibility and allow complex decision boundaries
To reduce the number of parameters needed
To enable parallel processing of multiple data points

questions.explanation: Data re-uploading interleaves data encoding with trainable layers multiple times, effectively increasing the model's expressibility. This allows the QNN to learn more complex decision boundaries even with few qubits.
